objective: Bootstrap worktree squad loops and retrospective-ready process.
whatWentWell: Clear separation of Samwise orchestration and squad-leader execution responsibilities.
Protected-main and isolated-worktree authorization rules were codified early.
whatDidNot: Calibration and loop-log tooling were initially missing and had to be added after qualification review.
openQuestions: How much process overhead will this introduce during very small tickets?
improvements: Run a calibration pass after one full assignment cycle.
Have Rosie mine diary and retrospective artifacts for recurring recommendations.
actionItems: Owner: Samwise - Run first full loop and close with retrospective at next checkpoint.
Owner: Rosie - Run retrospective gardening pass after first real loop closes.

objective: Close the current Taskboard parity checkpoint, capture participant learning, and identify the remaining AJ-required work before any mainline integration.
whatWentWell: Small bounded Taskboard parity slices produced visible improvements while keeping validation green.
Weak UI experiments were reverted instead of being forced into branch history.
The branch stayed clean and the active docs remained aligned with the real state of work.
whatDidNot: NS0 remained artifact-incomplete because a real interactive app session is still required.
Snapshot record mode still crossed a user-only approval boundary during execution.
openQuestions: After the real NS0 interaction run, does Taskboard need one more card-detail parity slice before AJ release review?
How should future checkpoints label artifact completeness more prominently than simple code completeness?
improvements: Separate pair-required work from agent-completable work at the start of each checkpoint.
Keep explicit blocker notes in active docs whenever a required artifact depends on real GUI interaction.
Use the first post-NS0 review to decide whether card-detail hierarchy is the last high-leverage parity gap.
actionItems: Owner: AJ + Samwise - Run the real NS0 interaction session and generate interaction-events.jsonl plus interaction-report.md.
Owner: Studio Interaction Quality Lead - Run a red-pen pass after NS0 closes.
Owner: Venture Product Steward - Produce a readiness memo after NS0 artifacts exist.
Owner: Rosie - Publish prioritized wrap-up recommendations for the next checkpoint.

objective: Garden the Taskboard wrap-up meeting artifacts and convert the checkpoint into evidence-backed recommendations for the next iteration.
whatWentWell: The checkpoint left a strong paper trail across commits, plan docs, partner logs, and retrospective artifacts.
The team explicitly recorded that NS0 is not done until real interaction artifacts exist.
Rejected experiments were treated as useful learning rather than hidden churn.
whatDidNot: Approval interruptions still surfaced during snapshot record mode.
The distinction between code completeness and artifact completeness had to be clarified late in the checkpoint.
openQuestions: Will AJ judge the branch mainline-ready after the NS0 pairing run or request one more detail-polish slice?
Should artifact-completeness become a required field in every future checkpoint closeout?
improvements: Add an artifact-completeness callout to future wrap-up packets.
Classify future checkpoint work as agent-completable versus pair-required before implementation begins.
Treat approval interruptions as first-class retrospective data in the delegated-authority experiment.
actionItems: Owner: AJ + Samwise - Complete the NS0 paired app run and verify generated telemetry artifacts.
Owner: Venture Product Steward - Issue a post-NS0 go/no-go readiness recommendation.
Owner: Samwise - Add artifact-completeness language to the next wrap-up checklist.

objective: Close the documentation-only workstream-routing canary and record whether the new artifact shape reduces route ambiguity.
keepDoing: Keep deriving route position from directive-owned workstream metadata instead of inventing a second routing source.
lessOf: Less reliance on single next-session fields without visible lifecycle context.
moreOf: More explicit route summaries in the artifacts operators actually read during planning, execution, and closeout.
stopDoing: Stop making operators reconstruct the worktree-squad lifecycle from memory or scattered adjacent docs.
startDoing: Start treating workstream routing as standard output for new planning, loop, and retrospective artifacts when the active directive carries workstream metadata.
actionItems: Owner: Samwise - Add the canary shape to the next real workstream-aware planning pass.
Owner: Worktree Squad Lead - Use the canary as the reference shape for the next real worktree loop and closeout.

objective: Close Trial 1 of the squad-pattern evaluation by reviewing the generated workstream-docs tranche and what it proved about squad execution.
keepDoing: Keep projecting operator routing docs from directive-owned workstream metadata instead of asking operators to maintain routing by hand.
Keep enforcing routing consistency through validation and check commands, not doc review alone.
lessOf: Less manual workstream annotation inside hybrid docs such as the session directory.
Less assumption that every medium-complexity task naturally decomposes into parallel squad lanes.
moreOf: More explicit generated/manual boundary contracts when one document stays hybrid.
More deterministic ordering and validation rules written down before generator work starts.
stopDoing: Stop relying on operator memory to reconstruct workstream membership or lifecycle position.
Stop treating role labels alone as proof that a task benefited from multi-lane execution.
startDoing: Start using squads first as a scoping and boundary-review tool, and only second as a parallel-delivery pattern when the work actually splits cleanly.
Start recording process limitations in the same closeout artifact as the shipped feature result.
actionItems: Owner: Samwise - Use this retrospective as Trial 1 baseline in the squad-pattern comparison memo.
Owner: Rosie - Mine this retrospective together with the delegated-grounding closeout for a reusable squad-selection heuristic.

details.featureOutcome: Generated docs reduced operator drift and improved routing/source-of-truth clarity.
details.processOutcome: Squad structure improved scope control, boundary review, and stop-point clarity.
details.processLimitation: Implementation remained largely sequential because the work wanted one shared core path.

objective: Close Trial 2 of the squad-pattern evaluation by reviewing the delegated-agent grounding refinement and what it proved about squads on process-contract work.
keepDoing: Keep using bounded squad passes to harden planning, template, and logging surfaces together when the behavior spans multiple contract layers.
Keep making fallback ladders explicit instead of leaving them in operator memory or one-off review comments.
lessOf: Less trust that slim prompts alone will preserve PersonaKit grounding for delegated lanes.
Less tendency to open new schema fields before the existing log shape proves insufficient.
moreOf: More contract-template-log triangulation when changing workflow behavior.
More explicit success criteria before process experiments start, so closeouts can judge the process fairly instead of narratively.
stopDoing: Stop leaving delegated-agent grounding as a planning habit instead of a planning-owned requirement.
Stop broadening contract-hardening passes into new artifact families before the first bounded pass lands and validates cleanly.
startDoing: Start requiring delegated handoff packets whenever spawned-agent roles are named in planning output.
Start scoring feature outcome and process outcome separately in contract-hardening retrospectives.
actionItems: Owner: Samwise - Use this trial as the main evidence for whether squads help on process-boundary work.
Owner: Pack Gardener - Keep delegated-handoff detail in the current planning-log shape unless recurring friction proves a top-level schema field is worth it.

details.featureOutcome: Planning-stack artifacts now require delegated handoff packets plus an MCP-first fallback ladder.
details.processOutcome: Squad structure materially helped on process-contract work that crossed planning review, active contracts, templates, log guidance, and maintenance discipline.
details.processLimitation: The pass still leaned sequential and would have been overkill for a simpler single-surface update.
